feedback.text: I would like to draw attention to the need for enhanced consumer protection regarding the purchase of digital content (video games, music, films, etc.). Today, many studios retain the right to revoke access to a purchased product, without justification and at any time, which is contrary to the ‘final’ purchase. For example, I recommend: Prohibition of arbitrary revocation of access rights to legally acquired digital content. Debligation, in particular for online or server-component video games, to propose an EOL plan: this could include community servers, offline mode, or opening the tools needed to preserve the game. This would ensure that consumers’ digital rights are respected in the same way as for tangible goods.
